data.title: AbbVie (NYSE: ABBV) declares $1.64 quarterly dividend per share, payable Nov. 14, 2025, to stockholders of record by Oct. 15.
data.description: In North Chicago, Illinois, on September 5, 2025, the board of directors of AbbVie Inc. (NYSE: ABBV) announced a quarterly cash dividend of $1.64 per share. This dividend is set to be paid on November 14, 2025, to shareholders of record as of October 15, 2025. This news highlights the company's commitment to rewarding its shareholders.

data.title: Dow Jones Futures up, Nvidia awaits earnings, Tesla breaks out, 5 stocks near buy points in aerospace and defense sector.
data.description: The stock market saw an increase as investors anticipated Nvidia's earnings report. Tesla's stock experienced a significant rise. Additionally, a number of aerospace and defense stocks are approaching potential buy points. Dow Jones Futures saw a rise as Nvidia's earnings loomed, with Tesla emerging as a new standout, alongside 5 stocks nearing potential buy points.

data.title: Justin Sun calls for unlocking of frozen WLFI tokens, warning of damage to Trump-linked platform's reputation.
data.description: Sun expressed frustration over his pre-sale allocation for WLFI being unexpectedly frozen, which he deemed as unjust and potentially harmful to the reputation of the decentralized finance platform associated with the Trump family.

data.title: Tether explores gold investments to diversify profits, considering opportunities in the gold supply chain. USDT market cap: $168 billion, earned $5.7 billion profit in the first half of the year.
data.description: Tether (CRYPTO: USDT) is considering investments in the gold industry to diversify its profits from cryptocurrency operations. According to reports, the stablecoin issuer has been in talks with mining groups and investment firms regarding opportunities in various aspects of the gold supply chain. With a market capitalization of $168 billion, Tether has seen substantial earnings, with profits reaching $5.7 billion in the first half of the year. These profits mainly stem from interest gained from U.S. Treasuries that secure the stablecoin. CEO Paolo Ardoino views gold as a comparable asset to Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) due to its historical role as a store of value.
